programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A model is electrocuted at a fashion show, and the CSIs quickly determine her death was no accident. Investigating further, they discover the case is connected to a heroin-smuggling ring involving a fashion designer - and a prisoner who once held Horatio's son Kyle hostage. Crime drama, starring David Caruso

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team tries to solve the murder of a teenage boy before his distraught parents take the law into their own hands and seek revenge on a street gang suspected of being responsible. A pocket watch provides Calleigh and Delko with a vital clue and an arrest is made - but the case has a tragic outcome. Former small-screen Superman Dean Cain guest stars in the crime drama

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: On her day off, Calleigh narrowly avoids becoming the victim of a carjacking by opening fire on her assailants. However, an error in her judgement causes the death of a bystander. As she faces a wrongful-death lawsuit, the detectives scour the scene of the incident in search of evidence that might clear her name. Crime drama, starring Emily Procter

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The young daughter of a wealthy family finds her nanny dead, setting in motion an investigation that sheds an unflattering light on the lives of the rich and famous. Horatio and the team suspect the victim may have been having an affair with her employer, giving him a motive for murder, but eventually follow a trail of evidence to another suspect

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team investigates the deaths of three arms smugglers killed in a warehouse by an illegal electronic super-weapon, and a gunrunner interviewed by Eric is shot during his questioning. However, the detectives' chief suspect uses anti-terrorist legislation known as the Patriot Act in a bid to throw them off track. Drama, starring David Caruso

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will returns to duty after recovering from injuries sustained in the explosion and immediately becomes a hero when he foils a suicide bid by long-suffering Louisa Durante. His moment of glory is tinged with disappointment on learning Emma is engaged to Matt, and he later discovers Dan is using steroids to boost his boxing prospects. Aware of the pressure on veterans Tony and Reg, Smithy gives them what seems to be a simple dog-napping case, but it's not long before Sun Hill has a murder inquiry on its hands

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Linden is distraught and struggles to control his emotions when he discovers graffiti on his wife's grave, and Jac is rushed into theatre when she suddenly falls victim to a dangerous lung condition. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jack is taken into custody after Bex spots him visiting Tony and maliciously reports his whereabouts to the police. Charlie offers Duffy a place to stay and Merlin receives a letter telling him his mother has been found

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Hospital-grade morphine appears on the streets of Sun Hill and is soon traced back to St Hugh's, where the doctor in charge of the internal investigation proves less than cooperative with the police. Tony pays his last respects as his father passes away peacefully

programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A diplomat is killed while attending the latest round of Middle East talks, and MI5 suspect a link with a shadowy group known as the November Committee - but become involved in a race against time when it appears a pro-Palestinian British MP is next on the assassins' hit list. The case is complicated further when a link is discovered between the organisation and Harry's daughter. Guest starring Anton Lesser and Corin Redgrave

Feature-length drama, guest starring Matthew McNulty and Jill Halfpenny, with Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby

programme.desc: Part one of two. Borough Commander Adam Okaro drops in and discovers all is not well in Sun Hill, with Gina and John at loggerheads over the zero-tolerance policy and the bad press it is generating. Kezia sets up a sting to get a confession from Beth Adamson's stalker Keith Durante, but the case takes a sinister turn when the frightened woman disappears under the nose of her plain-clothes police protector

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stalker Keith Durante is finally brought in for questioning after Kezia leans on his wife to retract the alibi she gave him. However, Jack Meadows finds the interrogation a frustrating affair and resorts to devious methods to extract a confession. A fire in a B&B alerts the Sun Hill watch to an illegal immigration operation on their patch, and Sam finally accepts she has no future with Stuart

programme.desc: A middle-aged widow's family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether he's a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

Sister Monica Joan goes into hospital for cataract surgery

Ken Stott stars in the crime drama, with Claire Price and Jennifer Black

programme.desc: Lynda La Plante's crime drama. programme.desc: Nikki struggles to come to terms with life back home and seeks out fellow pathologist Sally Vaughan for support, only to learn she is nowhere to be found. As Jack, Clarissa and Thomas share concerns about Nikki's wellbeing, a body found in a reservoir is linked to a murder case from 16 years previously. programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Alison and Janice love Australia and want a relaxing, Oz-inspired 'bolt hole', complete with cracked earth-beach look, water feature, palm trees and fire bowl in their Goring-by-Sea home, so the Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock head to West Sussex to see how they can help

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: An unusual piece of sporting history arrives for the attention of silversmith Brenton West and leather expert Suzie Fletcher - a decorative belt awarded to a Victorian speed walker. A 1950s metal mobo roundabout and a dented trumpet are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: A Question of Sport

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by radio presenter Sara Cox, comedian Jason Manford, journalist Nick Robinson and actor Harry Shearer, who aim to help the team captains deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda, Max and Sharon team up for Frankie's Drag Bingo at the Prince Albert, while Mick works behind the bar - worried about his wife being around so much booze. Mitch goes to see Gray but he doesn't get the response he was expecting

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy goes to extreme lengths to impress her psychologist boyfriend, much to Agnes's disgust, while Rory locates the perfect apartment for him and Dino - the problem is, it's in Kilbride, meaning he'll have to learn to drive. Mark prepares to emigrate to Australia. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Awkwafina stars as Nora Lin in this scripted comedy series based on her real-life beginnings in Queens, New York. Raised alongside her cousin by her father and grandmother, Nora leans on her family as she navigates young adulthood. In this opening episode, she takes her first steps towards financial independence in an attempt to move out of her childhood home

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora and Grandma go to Atlantic City, where Nora gambles and runs into an old acquaintance on the boardwalk. Meanwhile, Grandma and her friends battle to use a casino power outlet. Comedy, starring Awkwafina and Lori Tan Chinn

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A light-hearted look back at 15 years of the business challenge, beginning with some of the most unforgettable candidates. These are the wheeler-dealers, the walking egos, the sharp-suited entrepreneurs and the jokers who make the show what it is - from the sales patter of Paul back in series one and tenacious Ruth Badger 12 months later, right up to the barrow-boy banter of Thomas last year. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: A look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur and Leeds United v Manchester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Leeds Rhinos v Wigan Warriors (Kick-off 2.30pm). Coverage of the opening semi-final at the Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens, as the sides battle it out for a place in the Wembley final on October 17. The Rhinos comfortably defeated Hull Kingston Rovers in their quarter-finals, while the Warriors had a similarly smooth passage against Rovers' city rivals Hull FC

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood, Jenni Falconer, Nikki Fox, Susannah Streeter, Paul Ross, Nick Hancock, Mark Durden-Smith and Kate Thornton take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, teams from Watford, Glasgow and Newcastle take part

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade focus their attention on finding a girl vocal harmony group. The talented hopefuls are auditioned individually, before having to prove themselves alongside their competitors. Who will impress Little Mix enough to make the final line up?

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Celebrating the unforgettable highlights from the glitziest theme night of them all. Tess Daly, Claudia Winkleman and the whole cast are once again joined by some special guests to remember the captivating routines, the explosive pro numbers and the standout moments of the sparkliest show in town. And this week, the pros perform a new Musicals group dance - all filmed from their homes in lockdown - a unique piece of Strictly magic

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Return of the game show hosted by Danny Dyer, in which contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Sisters Nichola and Paula from Co Antrim take part in this first edition, facing tricky questions and big decisions as they bid to win money for their family's future. The questions are asked by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leeds United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Crystal Palace. Leeds have already been involved in some high-scoring matches on their return to the top flight, and they were set to face another test of their credentials against a City side looking to regain the title

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the latest talking points from the NFL and look ahead to the weekend's action, with fixtures including Dallas Cowboys v Cleveland Browns, Las Vegas Raiders v Buffalo Bills, and Kansas City Chiefs v New England Patriots

programme.title: West Is West (2010)
programme.desc: A traditionally minded Muslim father living in 1970s Salford decides the solution to his troubled youngest son's problems is to reconnect with his roots. He takes the reluctant teenager on a trip to Pakistan, only to have an awkward encounter with his first wife. Comedy sequel to East Is East, with Om Puri, Aqib Khan and Linda Bassett

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leeds United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Crystal Palace. Leeds have already been involved in some high-scoring matches on their return to the top flight, and they were set to face another test of their credentials against a City side looking to regain the title

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan introduces coverage of the elite men's race, with the field including world record holder and defending champion Eliud Kipchoge, and long-distance great Kenenisa Bekele. British four-time Olympic champion Sir Mo Farah is the pacemaker for this year's men's race, which will take place in a bio-secure closed course that will consist of 1.5 mile laps, taking in the iconic sights of The Mall, Horse Guards Parade and Buckingham Palace. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her journey of faith, from becoming a Christian as a teen to her ordination and beyond, as she visits the people, places and music that have shaped her

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Neil Wragg helping transform a sofa, some deckchairs and two tables

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The band search for a group who not only sing, but also play instruments, with the musicians auditioned individually and then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas pounds the streets in a desperate search for his son, while back in the UK Connie scours the internet for clues to Albie's whereabouts and finds one on Kat's social media account. Returning to the hotel, a defeated Douglas confides in fellow lone traveller Freja about his and Connie's past, but with still no sign of Albie, and after an unexpected brush with the law, he reaches rock bottom

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Sheffield United at the Emirates Stadium. Champions Liverpool were also in action as they visited Aston Villa, while newly promoted Fulham were also on their travels, away to Wolverhampton Wanderers. Plus, a round-up of all the goals and talking points from yesterday's fixtures, and a look at the morning's headlines on the back pages

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League including Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Chelsea

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: West Ham suffer an 8-0 defeat to Chelsea - the heaviest in the club's history - and captain Gilly Flaherty reveals her mental health struggles as a professional footballer

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a kids' drum kit from the 1920s that has been beaten into submission, a vintage oil lamp and a 100-year-old fret saw. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea repairs a memorial to First World War soldiers from the village of Norton, and a woman seeks help restoring a teddy bear that belonged to her late stepfather. Plus, Geoff Harvey gets to work on a German-built fruit machine and silversmith Brenton West takes on the repair of a precious solid silver trophy presented by the Royal Navy

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest matches, including Wigan Warriors v St Helens, Salford Red Devils v Warrington Wolves, and Huddersfield Giants v Hull Kingston Rovers

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ricky Wilson presents an appeal on behalf of the Teenage Cancer Trust, a national charity offering care and support services to young people living with cancer

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Nottingham as Carol Gardner and Jane Anderson use their own cash to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George is getting a lot of unexpected attention from the local women and things come to a head at Fiona's museum fundraiser. Arlo has a run-in at school with the McNamara family

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Patrick Grant and Esme Young set the seven remaining contestants the challenge of tackling techniques and garment styles from other cultures and continents. They begin with a hard-to-follow pattern for a Chinese qipao-style top that has an asymmetrical neckline, fiddly zip insertion and is made with a fabric that frays, then they are given the task of transforming saris into items of clothing with a distinctly Eastern flavour and by creating West Africa-inspired dresses. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Elephant Family & Me
programme.desc: The second of two programmes in which Gordon Buchanan encounters a family of African elephants in the wilderness of Kenya, and attempts to gain their trust so he can observe them up close in their natural habitat

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents a special edition, revisiting some of the historic venues from the series' history. Paul also finds out about the scandalous Georgians of Lydiard Park

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three passionate young farmers - the next generation of rising talent - battle it out at the Farmers Weekly Awards, hoping to be crowned Young Farmer of the Year

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: A pared-down approach to cuisine in which the domestic goddess reveals that what people cook and how they prepare it really can make them feel better and more alive. Nigella prepares her avocado toast with radishes for breakfast, a Thai noodle dish with cinnamon and prawns, and a laid-back family supper of seeded lamb ribs, served with a feta and avocado salad. Nigella also serves her warm-spiced cauliflowers and 'chicksalad' and a sweet treat in the form of an apricot almond cake

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The presenter enters the glitzy world of awards and prizes - for books, movies, as well as poetry, museums and gardens - examining how they influence what art is created and what culture society consumes. She also reflects on if the pandemic has helped people see beyond the glamour and red carpets and reassess what prizes are really for. Contributors include Emmy-nominated actor Brian Cox, and Bernardine Evaristo, who last year shared the Booker Prize with Margaret Atwood

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A look at the work of mountain vets at the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland. Cahir is racing to an urgent call-out from a local pedigree sheep breeder, whose prize ewe is having difficulty in labour, and has to perform an emergency C-section. Nuala diagnoses a deep-rooted infection, finding her only choice is to put the calf down or take on a challenging amputation. Meanwhile, Jennie sees a newborn lamb brought to the clinic by its dedicated young owner, farmer Stephen

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie drives into the mountains to help with a case that reminds her that being a Mountain Vet is anything but glamorous, as she has to lance a giant boil the size of a watermelon on the leg of a cow. Julie, Castle Vets' head nurse, has seen most things in her long career, but today is a first for her, as a rare wild animal has been brought in, found lying at the side of the road and before assessing what's wrong with it, she must first find out what it is

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don revels in the early autumn colour in his garden, reviews how the tomatoes he grew from a late sowing have got on this season and gives advice on harvesting and storing apples. Rachel de Thame reveals how the new garden project she started in the spring has developed over the summer and Fish, of the rock band Marillion, gives a tour of his garden and reveals how it has helped his mental health as well as the creative process of making music. Plus, in Germany, Arit Anderson finds out about the transformation of a disused industrial site into a thriving and beautiful park

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools welcomes guest Robert Plant, inviting the Led Zeppelin frontman to talk him through his musical journey, loves and influences, illustrated by classic clips from the show's archive. Plus, Declan McKenna joins Jools in the studio in a special performance at the piano of Be an Astronaut, from his recently released and critically acclaimed second album Zeros, a record heavily influenced by his '70s glam heroes. There's also music from London Grammar

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Baltar is dumbfounded when Number Six shows up in Galactica's CIC claiming to be a woman called Shelley Godfrey. He is even more distressed when she accuses him of being a traitor against humanity, Sci-fi drama, starring Edward James Olmos, James Callis and Tricia Helfer

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Kara feels guilty when she is ordered to torture a Cylon prisoner to find out where a bomb has been hidden on the Galactica. The crew members hear a nuclear warhead is poised to explode on board, but it takes time for them to believe the rumour is true

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi is quick to regret his decision to play a show at a college campus that Earn arranged. Comedy drama about a rapper and his manager

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: A flashback to Earn and Al's adolescence, where the authenticity of Earn's shirt is embarrassingly called into question at school

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn runs around town trying to finish everything before catching a plane so that his rapper cousin Alfred, aka Paper Boi, can go on a European tour with Clark County. Comedy drama, starring Donald Glover, about an aspiring music manager trying to turn his life around by making his rapper cousin a star

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme hears from whistleblowers working inside the Government's new coronavirus tracking system. They are so concerned about NHS Test and Trace that they are speaking out to reveal chaos, technical problems, confusion, wasted resources and a system that does not appear to them to be working. There are also interviews with members of local public health teams, who say they have largely been ignored by the Government in favour of the private companies hired to run the new centralised tracking system

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Arlene Stuart investigates the shocking increase in litter around Scottish beauty spots after lockdown and what is being done to tackle it. Anne Lundon looks back to a village once branded the dirtiest in Scotland, that campaigned to clean up the mess on their doorstep. Dougie Vipond finds out how to roam responsibly in the outdoors as a dog owner and teams up with Nick Nairn to celebrate the best from the natural larder, cooking wood pigeon outdoors on the shores of the Lake of Menteith

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood take a look at elks on the rampage in a quiet American town, a fish that can walk on dry land, and a strange breed of fainting goats

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield look back at all the amazing dens built throughout the series and reminisce the big reveals

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The most unusual places in the UK

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Richie Driss meets artist Venessa Scott, who is on a mission to create an epic mural to capture what it is like growing up in modern-day Britain. Also, inspirational Blue Peter fans reveal how they earned their Blue Peter badges

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve's search sees him chomp through a car like a T-rex, wield an axe like an Allosaurus and hitch a ride in Britain's biggest tank to showcase the Spinosaurus's size and power

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how gravity influences events across the universe, discovering why such a relatively weak force dictates Earth's orbit around the Sun - as well as the solar system's movement through the galaxy. He also explains why the gravity of a neutron star is much stronger than that of Earth, and looks back through history to reveal why scientists' research into the power of attraction has given mankind a far greater understanding of the cosmos

programme.title: Shark
programme.desc: A look at how scientists are challenging traditional perceptions of sharks, with breakthroughs regarding the creatures' intelligence and social skills presenting them in a surprising new light. The programme also highlights some researchers' beliefs that studying the predators could help to solve some of the most pressing medical challenges of the 21st century. Narrated by Paul McGann

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A look at the work of mountain vets at the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland. Cahir is racing to an urgent call-out from a local pedigree sheep breeder, whose prize ewe is having difficulty in labour, and has to perform an emergency C-section. Nuala diagnoses a deep-rooted infection, finding her only choice is to put the calf down or take on a challenging amputation. Meanwhile, Jennie sees a newborn lamb brought to the clinic by its dedicated young owner, farmer Stephen

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: At Tullyraine Equine Clinic, vet Hugh Suffern examines mare Echo who has a lame hind leg. Owner Alex trusts only Hugh with Echo's treatment, but its unclear exactly what's causing her lameness. Alex has spent years training Echo in Eventing and if Hugh is unsuccessful, her sporting career will be over. Elsewhere, Maurice delivers a valuable litter of 11 sniffer-dogs and Rachel operates on a therapy dog with possible cancerous skin growths. A local woman with a particular affinity with animals adopts an flightless jackdaw, hand feeding him and encouraging him to use his wings on short test flights

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by fulfilling a lifetime's ambition to visit Japan, the home of their favourite food. The duo sample sushi at Tokyo's famous fish market and have the privilege of spending time in a sumo stable, where they take part in a wrestling bout

programme.title: Eliud Kipchoge: My Sub Two Hour Marathon
programme.desc: An interview with the marathon world record-holder, who provides access to his training camp in Kenya, while those closest to him reveal what makes him so special

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The former Strictly judge takes ex-BBC journalist Jennie Bond on a stroll down memory lane to her favourite childhood holiday destination, Pendoggett in Cornwall. In an attempt to recapture 1966, Len picks Jennie up in a Humber saloon, almost identical to the one her family had. The duo head to the quaint hotel the former royal reporter stayed in during England's World Cup winning year, but it's not long before they have a go at one of the region's most popular activities - surfing

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Royal Marines Museum in Southsea, Hampshire, Katherine Higgins learns about the local corset industry and Charles Hanson hears all about cap ribbons worn by German navy men. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin hitches a ride on hovercraft to uncover the history of the clever craft that was designed, built, and is still used on the Solent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A West Yorkshire couple are slowly seeing an empty nest now their eldest two children have left home and wonder if the time has come to realise their dream of moving to New Zealand

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts travel the globe in search of the most surprising animal stories and reveal how and why animals do such remarkable things. The first episode explores the world of animal intelligence, with Hugh visiting Florida to investigate an unexpected relationship between an alligator and a manatee, which has animal intelligence experts baffled. Plus, conservationist Giles Clark finds out if elephants can feel empathy - one of the most sophisticated forms of emotional intelligence

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Salford Red Devils v Warrington Wolves (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the second semi-final at the Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. The Wolves came through the tie of the round in the quarter-finals, beating St Helens 20-18, while the Red Devils caused something of an upset by defeating high-flying Catalans Dragons in their encounter

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: James Crawford takes to the skies to explore Scotland's cities, coasts and countryside. He begins by discovering the origins of aerial photography itself and then takes a bird's-eye view of forgotten factories, abandoned villages and secret military installations

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: In the heart of south-east Asia lies the tropical island of Borneo. Twice the size of the British Isles, it is the third largest island on earth and home to possibly the greatest diversity of life of any island. Its huge variety of habitats, from bustling coral reefs and ancient jungles to towering mountains, have given rise to more than 60,000 species of plants and animals - many found nowhere else on Earth. This documentary covers Borneo's rich abundance of wildlife, from frogs to orang-utans

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig talks garbage as she asks Alan Davies and this week's guest panellists, Bridget Christie, Johnny Vegas and Holly Walsh, to reveal all they know about rubbish

programme.title: Yardie (2018)
programme.desc: Idris Elba directs this crime drama set in 1970s Kingston and 1980s Hackney. Troubled young man D grows up under the wing of a Kingston don and music producer, who dispatches him to London, where he reconnects with his childhood sweetheart. But before he can be convinced to abandon his life of crime. D encounters the man who shot his brother 10 years earlier and embarks on a bloody, explosive quest for retribution. Starring Aml Ameen, Fraser James and Sheldon Shepherd

programme.title: Golf: Scottish Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour introduces highlights of the third day from the Renaissance Club in north Berwick, where Austrian Bernd Wiesberger was victorious last year

programme.title: A Hard Day's Night (1964)
programme.desc: The Beatles, harassed by their manager and Paul's grandfather, embark on a train journey to London for a TV show, but find themselves dogged by all manner of problems, including the inevitable overexcited fans. Musical comedy, starring the Fab Four alongside Wilfrid Brambell, Victor Spinetti and Anna Quayle. Songs include Can't Buy Me Love, Don't Bother Me and the title track

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don revels in the early autumn colour in his garden, reviews how the tomatoes he grew from a late sowing have got on this season and gives advice on planting onions for next year. Rachel de Thame reveals how the new garden project she started in the spring has developed over the summer and Fish, of the rock band Marillion, gives a tour of his garden and reveals how it has helped his mental health as well as the creative process of making music. Plus, in Germany, Arit Anderson finds out about the transformation of a disused industrial site into a thriving and beautiful park

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the 40th London Marathon, live from The Mall, where the women's elite race is the first to get under way. The event takes place in very different circumstances this year, as the leading racers in the world will battle it out for the titles, while around 45,000 runners will take part in the ‘virtual' London Marathon. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary explores how families use food to create their own sense of home, including a father and daughter who have taken their home cooking to the streets with their own food van. Later in the programme, Mary makes some of her own family favourites, including a hearty bolognaise bake with a burrata and heritage tomato salad, and a paneer and roast vegetable curry

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents continued live coverage of the 40th London Marathon, featuring the men's and women's wheelchair races. This year's event involves elite athletes only due to the Coronavirus pandemic, but the day will see 45,000 runners from around the world run the 26.2 mile distance as part of the ‘virtual' London Marathon - and some of their own inspirational stories will be featured throughout the broadcast. With commentary by Andrew Cotter and Tanni Grey-Thompson

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by the Oscar-winning actor John Wayne, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career that led to him becoming considered a US national icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: The Searchers (1956)
programme.desc: An embittered American Civil War veteran sets out to find his niece, who has been abducted by a renegade Comanche tribe. His search takes him many years, and during the long quest his travelling companion starts to wonder if he intends to rescue her or kill her. John Ford's Western, starring John Wayne, Natalie Wood, Jeffrey Hunter and Vera Miles

programme.title: Golf: Scottish Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the final day's play from the Renaissance Club on the north Berwickshire coast, where Bernd Wiesberger triumphed last year, beating Benjamin Hebert in a play-off. Overnight leader Wiesberger finished two-under for the day and 22-under overall, with France's Hebert carding a stunning nine-under round of 62. However, it was the Austrian who triumphed in dramatic style on the third sudden-death hole after a three-putt bogey by Hebert

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan reveal how Birmingham's Victorian working class residents made their home city one of the most important industrial centres on the planet. They examine the surviving remains of one of the world's oldest railway terminuses, and discover how many of the thousands of skeletons in the cemetery have evidence of bone damage or amputation, as well as signs of the after-effects of industrial pollution. Plus, a look at the true story behind Peaky Blinders

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin revisits his travel series, beginning with Around the World in 80 Days. He draws on his personal archive of audio recordings and diary notebooks, and reflects on how he became a seasoned global traveller. The first episode revisits his visits to Venice, Cairo, Saudi Arabia, India, Hong Kong and China - a nation that was closed to most westerners at the time. Featuring contributions from fans and friends including David Attenborough, Joanna Lumley and Simon Reeve

programme.title: 25 Siblings & Me
programme.desc: The incredible story of Oli, a young man with Asperger's syndrome, who overnight discovered he has 25 siblings from the same American sperm donor dad. Desperate to be part of this new family, Oli travels to the USA, meeting his donor and joining a mass sibling reunion

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Wider family and friends start gathering at the Fox household to celebrate Frankie's 15th birthday with a 'Batceanera', a mixed cultural event shared with her friend Rosita

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam takes Duke and Murray to an LA Dodgers game, but has difficulty finding a ride home. Xander arrives for a family meal but gets a taste of his own medicine

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the 40th London Marathon, featuring the elite men's, women's and wheelchair races. Commentary by Steve Cram, Andrew Cotter and Tanni Grey-Thompson

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Sylar's murderous rampage continues and leads to a confrontation with Peter, while Nathan makes important decisions based on Linderman's shocking revelations. Thompson uses every means possible to hunt for Claire, and Hiro's determination to save the world grows stronger in the face of adversity. Guest starring Eric Roberts (The Dark Knight)

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The future holds disappointment for Hiro when he discovers that people with secret powers are considered terrorists. As the time-traveller prepares for a showdown with the President he confronts some familiar faces who have changed over the years

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the political debate, inviting a panel of guests from the worlds of politics, media and showbusiness to answer questions on subjects that have made the headlines over the past seven days

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Timothy Spall: Somewhere at Sea
programme.desc: Tim and Shane navigate their way through ports and islands on the west coast of Scotland, but the misty weather affords them little time for sight-seeing. They then sail the Princess Matilda along the Caledonian Canal to the east coast, where they search for a place to moor their barge for the winter

programme.title: Sounds of the Eighties
programme.desc: Showcasing a selection of musical performances from the BBC archives, featuring Echo and the Bunnymen, Michael Stipe, the Smiths, the Jesus and Mary Chain, the Cure, Lone Justice and the Pixies

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Musical recap of a new decade and a new era as hip-hop, dance and indie flavours began to find a place in the mainstream charts. Top of the Pops continued to cater the best performances every Thursday to the British public with lots of dry ice, handheld cameras and a small but noisy studio audience. This compilation celebrates with some of the best studio performances of the biggest hits including break-out appearances by Adamski featuring Seal, Vanilla Ice, Beats International, EMF, the KLF Primal Scream and Snap! Plus, plenty of girl power from Betty Boo, Sinead O'Connor, Maria McKee, En Vogue, Kylie Minogue and Tina Turner

programme.title: Top of the Pops: The Story of 1990
programme.desc: A look back at the musical tastes of the early 1990s, when the new pop grammars of hip-hop and dance bewildered the old school institution that was the BBC's weekly chart show. Adamski, Orbital, 808 State and Snap! struggled to translate their brand of cool beats to the BBC's need for musicianship and random dancers, while the likes of Betty Boo, MC Tunes and Beats International brought the British take on hip-hop to the studio. In a year in that even saw footballer John Barnes embrace rap, these breakthrough hip-hop artists had to share the limelight with big balladeers including Sinead O'Connor and Maria McKee

programme.title: Geri's 1990s: My Drive to Freedom
programme.desc: Geri Horner follows in the footsteps of Tom Jones, Keith Richards, Boy George and Jazzie B by revisiting the decade that has defined her life and career to date. For Geri, that decade is the 1990s, when she shot to international stardom as Ginger Spice. At the height of her Spice Girls fame, Geri helped to ensure 'Girl Power' dominated the 'Cool Britannia' scene with her iconic Union Jack dress, and here she reflects on how a working-class girl from Watford came to symbolise the gender politics of a turbulent era. She also discusses the key political and social moments of the '90s, including Nelson Mandela's release, the birth of New Labour, the launch of the World Wide Web, and the death of Diana, Princess of Wales. Includes contributions by Geri's friends and family, and other media personalities from the decade

programme.title: Toots and the Maytals: Reggae Got Soul
programme.desc: A profile of Toots Hibbert, featuring an interview with the reggae musician and archive footage of his performances. The film traces his career from his early days as a singer in a Jamaican church to becoming a Grammy award-winning artist, and features contributions by Eric Clapton, Keith Richards, Jimmy Cliff, Bonnie Raitt, Willie Nelson, Marcia Griffiths and Paolo Nutini

programme.title: Toots and the Maytals at Glastonbury 2010
programme.desc: The ska group's performance on the West Holts Stage, featuring songs such as Pressure Drop, Louie Louie, Monkey Man and 54-46 Was My Number. Introduced by Mark Radcliffe

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm

programme.title: Top of the Pops: The Story of 1990
programme.desc: A look back at the musical tastes of the early 1990s, when the new pop grammars of hip-hop and dance bewildered the old school institution that was the BBC's weekly chart show. Adamski, Orbital, 808 State and Snap! struggled to translate their brand of cool beats to the BBC's need for musicianship and random dancers, while the likes of Betty Boo, MC Tunes and Beats International brought the British take on hip-hop to the studio. In a year in that even saw footballer John Barnes embrace rap, these breakthrough hip-hop artists had to share the limelight with big balladeers including Sinead O'Connor and Maria McKee

programme.title: Sounds of the Eighties
programme.desc: Showcasing a selection of musical performances from the BBC archives, featuring Echo and the Bunnymen, Michael Stipe, the Smiths, the Jesus and Mary Chain, the Cure, Lone Justice and the Pixies

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts how the first Venetians inhabited the lagoon in the fifth century AD and created an almost impregnable location, which took the shape of the Venice known today through pioneering engineering work. He reveals the stunning interiors of the Doge's Palace, the Basilica of St Mark and the Ca da Mosto, and uncovers a secret history - beset by violence and political intrigue

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin's journey through southern Africa is beset by minor mishaps. A spirit healer claims an evil shadow has descended upon him and he later suffers an unfortunate accident while white-water rafting at the Victoria Falls. However, these events are overshadowed by the shocking news he receives in Johannesburg - there are no places available on the annual sea voyage to Antarctica

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Mary is in peril as she is being used as bait, but Jay struggles to keep her safe as the threat closes in on both of them. Fran feels betrayed as she digs deeper into Zoe's disappearance

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Jay is lured into a trap when he tries to rescue Mary and an epic gun battle erupts, while Fran confronts the locals who have been implicated in past crimes

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane -to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from New York
programme.desc: Clive reluctantly revisits the Big Apple after being mugged there on a previous trip, so he takes a quick lesson in self-defence from a policewoman before hitting the mean streets of Manhattan and interviewing famous residents including Ivana Trump

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Musical recap of a new decade and a new era as hip-hop, dance and indie flavours began to find a place in the mainstream charts. Top of the Pops continued to cater the best performances every Thursday to the British public with lots of dry ice, handheld cameras and a small but noisy studio audience. This compilation celebrates with some of the best studio performances of the biggest hits including break-out appearances by Adamski featuring Seal, Vanilla Ice, Beats International, EMF, the KLF Primal Scream and Snap! Plus, plenty of girl power from Betty Boo, Sinead O'Connor, Maria McKee, En Vogue, Kylie Minogue and Tina Turner

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin's journey through southern Africa is beset by minor mishaps. A spirit healer claims an evil shadow has descended upon him and he later suffers an unfortunate accident while white-water rafting at the Victoria Falls. programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts how the first Venetians inhabited the lagoon in the fifth century AD and created an almost impregnable location, which took the shape of the Venice known today through pioneering engineering work. He reveals the stunning interiors of the Doge's Palace, the Basilica of St Mark and the Ca da Mosto, and uncovers a secret history - beset by violence and political intrigue

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: A year in the life of Bodnant Garden in North Wales. With visitor numbers in decline, head gardener Troy Scott Smith and his manager Michael McLaren are spending £2million on a revamp, with the aim of creating one of the top 10 gardens in the world

programme.title: Vienna Summer Night Concert 2020
programme.desc: The Vienna Philharmonic's annual Summer Night Concert takes place in Vienna's Schonbrunn Palace Gardens. Postponed from May, the 2020 show is conducted by Russian maestro Valery Gergiev and features German tenor Jonas Kaufmann. Adopting the theme of love, it features arias by Jules Massenet, Emmerich Kalman and Giacomo Puccini

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Days
programme.desc: Michael Palin sets off from London en route to Italy for the first leg of his attempt to emulate the legendary adventures of Phileas Fogg. After an overnight stay in Venice, the intrepid explorer helps the local refuse collectors before boarding the Espresso Egitto ferry bound for Alexandria

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Days
programme.desc: The ferry Michael Palin hopes to catch in Suez to take him across the Red Sea develops engine trouble and the next ship out is the following day. Missing his connection in Jeddah, he is granted permission to drive across the desert of Saudi Arabia - without his film crew - in an attempt to get his journey back on schedule

programme.title: The Hector: From Scotland to Nova Scotia
programme.desc: Neil Oliver recounts the story of the 1773 highland migrants who left Scotland to settle in Nova Scotia. He uncovers their terrifying journey in a filthy disease-ridden ship, the Hector, and goes on to meet some of their descendants

programme.title: Munro: Mountain Man
programme.desc: The story of Victorian adventurer Hugh Munro and his determination to list and climb all the Scottish mountains whose height exceeds 3,000ft. Nicholas Crane visits some of the country's spectacular mountains as he explores the adventurer's bid to reach more than 500 peaks and reveals how his name has been adopted by climbing enthusiasts pursuing the same goal, a pastime known as Munro bagging

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Documentary using bodycam and CCTV footage to tell the stories of the emergency service workers assaulted in the line of duty

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Today's auction property-developing projects take an incredible 14 days to more than two and a half years to complete. Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander visit the properties in Sandgate in Kent and Bolton in Lancashire as well as a plot of land in Enfield in north London at the start of the projects, following the progress until they're finished

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A fraudster has his expensive Range Rover taken off him to be sold at auction and police step in when they suspect an elderly man's home has been taken over by drug dealers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks back at some of the most eclectic sporting items bought by the teams, and offers advice on how to spot a bargain. The experts also take part in a special Bargain Hunt Games

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The mining village of Trefelin prepares for the annual summer fete, while Kamini goes in search of Dan, only to discover more than she bargained for. Megan pleads with Prem to stay and help the miners fight for justice, leading to a confrontation with Sharpe. Drama, starring Ayesha Dharker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of sugar-rich foods in Britain, and try to make their own versions of their favourite childhood sweets. The duo also prepare a cheesecake and a sweet-and-sour dish

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits Suffolk on a grand property search with a couple hot-footing it from the other side of the world to set down roots in the British countryside for their £900,000 budget. While in the county, Jules meets a dynamic dairy farming family whose customers are queuing up to buy their fresh milk straight from the farm's vending machine

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Alison and Janice love Australia and want a relaxing, Oz-inspired 'bolt hole', complete with cracked earth-beach look, water feature, palm trees and fire bowl in their Goring-by-Sea home, so the Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock head to West Sussex to see how they can help

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: An unusual piece of sporting history arrives for the attention of silversmith Brenton West and leather expert Suzie Fletcher - a decorative belt awarded to a Victorian speed walker. A 1950s metal mobo roundabout and a dented trumpet are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By Plaid Cymru

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer explores the hidden pieces of history from the Second World War. Wales made a huge contribution to the war effort and the remote countryside was witness to many military activities. Remnants from that time can still be found, but they lie hidden. Will seeks some of them out by visiting plane crash sites on the Brecon Beacons, uncovering an old training academy on the Great Orme, gaining exclusive access to an MoD training base that played a vital role in the conflict and tracks down the secret bunkers of a home-based army of saboteurs

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda, Max and Sharon team up for Frankie's Drag Bingo at the Prince Albert, while Mick works behind the bar - worried about his wife being around so much booze. Mitch goes to see Gray but he doesn't get the response he was expecting

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Rhod thinks he has a great chance of becoming a model - after all, he was voted Wales' 14th sexiest man. However, his dreams of strutting along catwalks in Milan and Paris are shattered by unimpressed fashion guru Julien Macdonald, so he has to lower his expectations somewhat, taking him into parts of the industry he never knew existed. He strips off for a room full of budding artists, meets a woman whose hands are her fortune, and signs up with an agency that focuses on 'character' rather than good looks. Can he make it or must he face the brutal truth?

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is proud of her working-class roots, but with the gap between rich and poor widening, she wants to know how easy it would be for her to move up a class. She overhauls her leisure activities with a spot of golf and some classical music, takes voice lessons to refine her accent and supercharges her table manners with an intense etiquette lesson. However, she soon discovers that there are obstacles in the way that can make social mobility very difficult

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Awkwafina stars as Nora Lin in this scripted comedy series based on her real-life beginnings in Queens, New York. Raised alongside her cousin by her father and grandmother, Nora leans on her family as she navigates young adulthood. In this opening episode, she takes her first steps towards financial independence in an attempt to move out of her childhood home

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora and Grandma go to Atlantic City, where Nora gambles and runs into an old acquaintance on the boardwalk. Meanwhile, Grandma and her friends battle to use a casino power outlet. Comedy, starring Awkwafina and Lori Tan Chinn

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A light-hearted look back at 15 years of the business challenge, beginning with some of the most unforgettable candidates. These are the wheeler-dealers, the walking egos, the sharp-suited entrepreneurs and the jokers who make the show what it is - from the sales patter of Paul back in series one and tenacious Ruth Badger 12 months later, right up to the barrow-boy banter of Thomas last year. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the fourth episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite chocolate recipes, including her 'Money Can't Buy You Happiness' brownies, a chocolate flan, and snack sized madeleines

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: A look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur and Leeds United v Manchester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Tim Weeks and Colin Young help two teams find items at an antique centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp learns about the history of local landscape with artist David Walker Barker

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Leeds Rhinos v Wigan Warriors (Kick-off 2.30pm). Coverage of the opening semi-final at the Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens, as the sides battle it out for a place in the Wembley final on October 17. The Rhinos comfortably defeated Hull Kingston Rovers in their quarter-finals, while the Warriors had a similarly smooth passage against Rovers' city rivals Hull FC

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood, Jenni Falconer, Nikki Fox, Susannah Streeter, Paul Ross, Nick Hancock, Mark Durden-Smith and Kate Thornton take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, teams from Watford, Glasgow and Newcastle take part

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade focus their attention on finding a girl vocal harmony group. The talented hopefuls are auditioned individually, before having to prove themselves alongside their competitors. Who will impress Little Mix enough to make the final line up?

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Celebrating the unforgettable highlights from the glitziest theme night of them all. Tess Daly, Claudia Winkleman and the whole cast are once again joined by some special guests to remember the captivating routines, the explosive pro numbers and the standout moments of the sparkliest show in town. And this week, the pros perform a new Musicals group dance - all filmed from their homes in lockdown - a unique piece of Strictly magic

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Return of the game show hosted by Danny Dyer, in which contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Sisters Nichola and Paula from Co Antrim take part in this first edition, facing tricky questions and big decisions as they bid to win money for their family's future. The questions are asked by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leeds United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Crystal Palace. Leeds have already been involved in some high-scoring matches on their return to the top flight, and they were set to face another test of their credentials against a City side looking to regain the title

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the latest talking points from the NFL and look ahead to the weekend's action, with fixtures including Dallas Cowboys v Cleveland Browns, Las Vegas Raiders v Buffalo Bills, and Kansas City Chiefs v New England Patriots

programme.title: West Is West (2010)
programme.desc: A traditionally minded Muslim father living in 1970s Salford decides the solution to his troubled youngest son's problems is to reconnect with his roots. He takes the reluctant teenager on a trip to Pakistan, only to have an awkward encounter with his first wife. Comedy sequel to East Is East, with Om Puri, Aqib Khan and Linda Bassett

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leeds United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Crystal Palace. Leeds have already been involved in some high-scoring matches on their return to the top flight, and they were set to face another test of their credentials against a City side looking to regain the title

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan introduces coverage of the elite men's race, with the field including world record holder and defending champion Eliud Kipchoge, and long-distance great Kenenisa Bekele. British four-time Olympic champion Sir Mo Farah is the pacemaker for this year's men's race, which will take place in a bio-secure closed course that will consist of 1.5 mile laps, taking in the iconic sights of The Mall, Horse Guards Parade and Buckingham Palace. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news and debate from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her journey of faith, from becoming a Christian as a teen to her ordination and beyond, as she visits the people, places and music that have shaped her

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Nurse Leanne responds to a medical emergency when a pupil has a seizure and is having difficulty breathing, while hairdresser Claire volunteers her time in the school's salon to work with pupils who find it extremely difficult and sometimes painful to have their hair

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer explores the hidden pieces of history from the Second World War. Wales made a huge contribution to the war effort and the remote countryside was witness to many military activities. Remnants from that time can still be found, but they lie hidden. Will seeks some of them out by visiting plane crash sites on the Brecon Beacons, uncovering an old training academy on the Great Orme, gaining exclusive access to an MoD training base that played a vital role in the conflict and tracks down the secret bunkers of a home-based army of saboteurs

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: Having exchanged modern conveniences for the harsh realities of life in a mining community of the 1920s, the mothers find themselves working 14-hour days while the men toil at the coal face and the children tackle a new curriculum at school. Narrated by Nicola Reynolds

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The band search for a group who not only sing, but also play instruments, with the musicians auditioned individually and then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas pounds the streets in a desperate search for his son, while back in the UK Connie scours the internet for clues to Albie's whereabouts and finds one on Kat's social media account. Returning to the hotel, a defeated Douglas confides in fellow lone traveller Freja about his and Connie's past, but with still no sign of Albie, and after an unexpected brush with the law, he reaches rock bottom

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Sheffield United at the Emirates Stadium. Champions Liverpool were also in action as they visited Aston Villa, while newly promoted Fulham were also on their travels, away to Wolverhampton Wanderers. Plus, a round-up of all the goals and talking points from yesterday's fixtures, and a look at the morning's headlines on the back pages

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League including Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Chelsea

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: West Ham suffer an 8-0 defeat to Chelsea - the heaviest in the club's history - and captain Gilly Flaherty reveals her mental health struggles as a professional footballer

programme.desc: A scam artist faking disability to claim undeserved payments. Plus, the activities of a prolific dental fraudster. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.desc: A round-up of political news and events at Westminster and Holyrood.

programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Arlene Stuart investigates the shocking increase in litter around Scottish beauty spots after lockdown and what is being done to tackle it. Anne Lundon looks back to a village once branded the dirtiest in Scotland, that campaigned to clean up the mess on their doorstep. Dougie Vipond finds out how to roam responsibly in the outdoors as a dog owner and teams up with Nick Nairn to celebrate the best from the natural larder, cooking wood pigeon outdoors on the shores of the Lake of Menteith

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy goes to extreme lengths to impress her psychologist boyfriend, much to Agnes's disgust, while Rory locates the perfect apartment for him and Dino - the problem is, it's in Kilbride, meaning he'll have to learn to drive. Mark prepares to emigrate to Australia. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six
