programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The band search for a group who not only sing, but also play instruments, with the musicians auditioned individually and then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas pounds the streets in a desperate search for his son, while back in the UK Connie scours the internet for clues to Albie's whereabouts and finds one on Kat's social media account. Returning to the hotel, a defeated Douglas confides in fellow lone traveller Freja about his and Connie's past, but with still no sign of Albie, and after an unexpected brush with the law, he reaches rock bottom

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Sheffield United at the Emirates Stadium. Champions Liverpool were also in action as they visited Aston Villa, while newly promoted Fulham were also on their travels, away to Wolverhampton Wanderers. Plus, a round-up of all the goals and talking points from yesterday's fixtures, and a look at the morning's headlines on the back pages

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest matches from the Scottish Premiership, which included Aberdeen v St Mirren, Rangers v Ross County and St Johnstone v Celtic

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League including Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Chelsea

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: West Ham suffer an 8-0 defeat to Chelsea - the heaviest in the club's history - and captain Gilly Flaherty reveals her mental health struggles as a professional footballer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Documentary using bodycam and CCTV footage to tell the stories of the emergency service workers assaulted in the line of duty

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Today's auction property-developing projects take an incredible 14 days to more than two and a half years to complete. Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander visit the properties in Sandgate in Kent and Bolton in Lancashire as well as a plot of land in Enfield in north London at the start of the projects, following the progress until they're finished

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A fraudster has his expensive Range Rover taken off him to be sold at auction and police step in when they suspect an elderly man's home has been taken over by drug dealers

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: A scam artist faking disability to claim undeserved payments. Plus, the activities of a prolific dental fraudster. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks back at some of the most eclectic sporting items bought by the teams, and offers advice on how to spot a bargain. The experts also take part in a special Bargain Hunt Games

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The mining village of Trefelin prepares for the annual summer fete, while Kamini goes in search of Dan, only to discover more than she bargained for. Megan pleads with Prem to stay and help the miners fight for justice, leading to a confrontation with Sharpe. Drama, starring Ayesha Dharker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of sugar-rich foods in Britain, and try to make their own versions of their favourite childhood sweets. The duo also prepare a cheesecake and a sweet-and-sour dish

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits Suffolk on a grand property search with a couple hot-footing it from the other side of the world to set down roots in the British countryside for their £900,000 budget. While in the county, Jules meets a dynamic dairy farming family whose customers are queuing up to buy their fresh milk straight from the farm's vending machine

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Alison and Janice love Australia and want a relaxing, Oz-inspired 'bolt hole', complete with cracked earth-beach look, water feature, palm trees and fire bowl in their Goring-by-Sea home, so the Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock head to West Sussex to see how they can help

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: An unusual piece of sporting history arrives for the attention of silversmith Brenton West and leather expert Suzie Fletcher - a decorative belt awarded to a Victorian speed walker. A 1950s metal mobo roundabout and a dented trumpet are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: The road racer was born within a stone's throw of the Tandragee road racing circuit, but little did he know as he watched motorbikes speed past him as a child that he'd go on to become one of the UK's top road racers. In his bid to life at 200 miles an hour, Phillip has broken his back and been in several comas. Here, he talks to Stephen Watson about his life and times

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda, Max and Sharon team up for Frankie's Drag Bingo at the Prince Albert, while Mick works behind the bar - worried about his wife being around so much booze. Mitch goes to see Gray but he doesn't get the response he was expecting

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Awkwafina stars as Nora Lin in this scripted comedy series based on her real-life beginnings in Queens, New York. Raised alongside her cousin by her father and grandmother, Nora leans on her family as she navigates young adulthood. In this opening episode, she takes her first steps towards financial independence in an attempt to move out of her childhood home

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora and Grandma go to Atlantic City, where Nora gambles and runs into an old acquaintance on the boardwalk. Meanwhile, Grandma and her friends battle to use a casino power outlet. Comedy, starring Awkwafina and Lori Tan Chinn

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A light-hearted look back at 15 years of the business challenge, beginning with some of the most unforgettable candidates. These are the wheeler-dealers, the walking egos, the sharp-suited entrepreneurs and the jokers who make the show what it is - from the sales patter of Paul back in series one and tenacious Ruth Badger 12 months later, right up to the barrow-boy banter of Thomas last year. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the fourth episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite chocolate recipes, including her 'Money Can't Buy You Happiness' brownies, a chocolate flan, and snack sized madeleines

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: A look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur and Leeds United v Manchester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Tim Weeks and Colin Young help two teams find items at an antique centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp learns about the history of local landscape with artist David Walker Barker

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Leeds Rhinos v Wigan Warriors (Kick-off 2.30pm). Coverage of the opening semi-final at the Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens, as the sides battle it out for a place in the Wembley final on October 17. The Rhinos comfortably defeated Hull Kingston Rovers in their quarter-finals, while the Warriors had a similarly smooth passage against Rovers' city rivals Hull FC

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood, Jenni Falconer, Nikki Fox, Susannah Streeter, Paul Ross, Nick Hancock, Mark Durden-Smith and Kate Thornton take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, teams from Watford, Glasgow and Newcastle take part

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade focus their attention on finding a girl vocal harmony group. The talented hopefuls are auditioned individually, before having to prove themselves alongside their competitors. Who will impress Little Mix enough to make the final line up?

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Celebrating the unforgettable highlights from the glitziest theme night of them all. Tess Daly, Claudia Winkleman and the whole cast are once again joined by some special guests to remember the captivating routines, the explosive pro numbers and the standout moments of the sparkliest show in town. And this week, the pros perform a new Musicals group dance - all filmed from their homes in lockdown - a unique piece of Strictly magic

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Return of the game show hosted by Danny Dyer, in which contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Sisters Nichola and Paula from Co Antrim take part in this first edition, facing tricky questions and big decisions as they bid to win money for their family's future. The questions are asked by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leeds United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Crystal Palace. Leeds have already been involved in some high-scoring matches on their return to the top flight, and they were set to face another test of their credentials against a City side looking to regain the title

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the latest talking points from the NFL and look ahead to the weekend's action, with fixtures including Dallas Cowboys v Cleveland Browns, Las Vegas Raiders v Buffalo Bills, and Kansas City Chiefs v New England Patriots

programme.title: West Is West (2010)
programme.desc: A traditionally minded Muslim father living in 1970s Salford decides the solution to his troubled youngest son's problems is to reconnect with his roots. He takes the reluctant teenager on a trip to Pakistan, only to have an awkward encounter with his first wife. Comedy sequel to East Is East, with Om Puri, Aqib Khan and Linda Bassett

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leeds United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Crystal Palace. Leeds have already been involved in some high-scoring matches on their return to the top flight, and they were set to face another test of their credentials against a City side looking to regain the title

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan introduces coverage of the elite men's race, with the field including world record holder and defending champion Eliud Kipchoge, and long-distance great Kenenisa Bekele. British four-time Olympic champion Sir Mo Farah is the pacemaker for this year's men's race, which will take place in a bio-secure closed course that will consist of 1.5 mile laps, taking in the iconic sights of The Mall, Horse Guards Parade and Buckingham Palace. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her journey of faith, from becoming a Christian as a teen to her ordination and beyond, as she visits the people, places and music that have shaped her

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Neil Wragg helping transform a sofa, some deckchairs and two tables

programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of five family members answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Rif, Tia, Keh and Lee work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bear worries about his son, Liv is pleasantly surprised, and Mandy is evasive

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A consultant tells Steve and Leanne that Oliver's condition is so severe they're unable to offer him treatment. Meanwhile, Nick secretly bonds with his son, Sam. Billy discovers Paul has been training at a helpline for teenagers, and Grace suggests to Michael they should move to Spain together

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is found buried on the Northumberland moors which could be a missing teenager from the nearby rural community of Brindale. The girl had disappeared six weeks previously, and Vera has to break the news to her devastated family. However, the realisation that she died so close to home rather than at a music festival as originally thought creates tension among her neighbours, who are soon suspecting each other of murder. The detective delves into the vulnerable young woman's isolated existence to identify her killer

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club
programme.desc: Jonathan and co-host Mawaan Rizwan present another stand-up showcase featuring some of the best and funniest new comedians in the UK. This episode features sets from Lloyd Griffith, Joanne McNally and Kemah Bob, while Tom Allen will be trying out some new material

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Host Holly Willoughby and team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh are joined by Aston Villa and former England footballer John Terry, Paralympic gold medallist Jonnie Peacock and Countdown star Rachel Riley. Comedians Romesh Ranganathan and Seann Walsh are on hand too

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's on a trip to Translovenia and takes shelter in a mansion owned by a vampire. In reality, this vampire is just Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: A bear has escaped from the zoo and Fizz is hell-bent on making it his pet. Magoo mistakes the bear for his niece who's come to spend the day with him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Fizz for his best friend Harry from school, and thinks the two of them are visiting their old school.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A businessman and his personal assistant decide to mark Halley's comet's latest orbit past Earth by flying their exclusive flying yacht beyond the atmosphere to chase it. Moments later, the pair send out a distress call as they find themselves trapped in the comet's tail, prompting Alan and Scott to take Thunderbird 3 out to tow the thrill-seekers to safety. Action adventure, with the voice of Rasmus Hardiker

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Fast flowing waters await Danny in today's dare as he takes on a white-water rafting course including the UK's steepest man-made drop.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Tom enlists the help of the lake monster to terrorize the mouse village forcing Jerry Van Mouseling to intervene and save the day

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis wins employee of the month and the right to share breakfast with Aunt Lydia

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: Scooby and the Mystery Inc. gang meet Wanda Sykes, who is helping save a bunch of cute, fuzzy animals up for adoption.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The TV chef is joined by guest Kirsty Gallacher, and chefs Thomasina Miers and Francesco Mazzei, and heads off on a food adventure to Kent. This week's recipes include langoustines, buttermilk fried chicken, mussels and sole, and a masterclass in celeriac

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott puts his own twist on the Great British teatime, serving up black forest brownies with kirsch cream, cheesy chive and bacon muffins and savoury afternoon tea tartlets. He is joined in the kitchen by John Partridge, who bakes some sausage rolls, and Candice Brown, who prepares gin and tonic drizzle cake and rocky road cookies

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's culinary tour of France leads him to the Camargue, a distinct and diverse region located south of Arles between the Mediterranean and the two arms of the Rhone delta. James begins his exploration at Les Marquises, the Laurent family's farm, where he learns all about the Camargue bull. He then barbeques a local fish, which he serves with squash chutney for the family. Later, Michelin-starred chef Armand Arnal shows James around his impressive gardens and lets him pick ingredients for his next dish - stuffed courgette flowers

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of this afternoon's meetings, featuring eight races in total. The feature races of the day are the 2.25 Cumberland Lodge Stakes from Ascot, the 2.45 Sun Chariot Stakes from Newmarket, the 3.05 bet365 Challenge Cup from Ascot, the 3.25 Trophy Stakes from Redcar and the 3.40 Bengough Stakes from Ascot. With analysis by Jason Weaver, reports from Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Rishi Persad, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the show's most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shepherd, in which the the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara return with the obstacle-course challenge, in which competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage though a series of outlandish physical trials. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with Krishnan Guru-Murphy, Lesley Joseph and Nathan Byron all trying to say what they see as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts this compilation of the arcade-themed game show's most dramatic finales, in which the last contestant standing tries to get the golden jackpot counter from the top shelf down to the win zone

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes actors Lesley Joseph and Hugh Dennis to his Hampshire farm and takes a look back to the allotment where his passion for gardening began. He also meets upholsterer Micaela Sharp and renowned garden photographer Clive Nichols. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford explore the secrets of Highclere Castle in Hampshire - which will be familiar to fans of Downton Abbey. Stories include the business of brewing cider at the Thatcher family's Somerset orchard and a visit to the north east coast to learn about the past and present of Holy Island oysters

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present live coverage from Paris, as two of the top horses in the world, Enable and Love, go head to head in the Prix De L'Arc de Triomphe. Enable, who won this race in 2017 and 2018, has been primed for a third win after her defeat 12 months ago, while Love has taken all before her this year with wins at Newmarket, Epsom and York

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Tim, Lorna, Hayley and Adam work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace, comedian Chris Ramsey and model and presenter Vogue Williams take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees two families trying to guess the answers to survey-based questions posed to 100 people. The family who score the most points go through to Big Money, which sees two of them answering just five questions for the £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: While the Blacketts conspire against him, Matthew learns the true nature of life and love in Singapore. As he and Vera get closer, Walter petitions the Governor and Joan hatches a plan of her own. Meanwhile, the Japanese tighten their grip on the continent. Second World War drama, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Elizabeth Tan and Georgia Blizzard

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. It's the start of the new year, and after their busy festive period, the hotel has high hopes for 2020 - looking at how they can improve all areas of their service and make it the most successful year in the hotel's history. Head butler Sean is determined to make his newest recruit - 37-year-old former actor Michael - the perfect Savoy butler, and is putting him to the test. As well as having to juggle a multitude of requests, Sean wants Michael to host an exclusive cocktail party for regular VIP guests, Mr and Mrs Cuming, in their suite

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy the northern white-cheeked gibbon is missing the visitors and wondering when they will be back to see him again, while keepers Mick and Joe use the quiet time to build the confidence of rare Bactrian camel Genghis, helping him to venture outside of his paddock. The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Brands Hatch, including the British F4 Championship and the Mini Dunlop Challenge

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bear worries about his son, Liv is pleasantly surprised, and Mandy is evasive

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A consultant tells Steve and Leanne that Oliver's condition is so severe they're unable to offer him treatment. Meanwhile, Nick secretly bonds with his son, Sam. Billy discovers Paul has been training at a helpline for teenagers, and Grace suggests to Michael they should move to Spain together

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is found buried on the Northumberland moors which could be a missing teenager from the nearby rural community of Brindale. The girl had disappeared six weeks previously, and Vera has to break the news to her devastated family. However, the realisation that she died so close to home rather than at a music festival as originally thought creates tension among her neighbours, who are soon suspecting each other of murder. The detective delves into the vulnerable young woman's isolated existence to identify her killer

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club
programme.desc: Jonathan and co-host Mawaan Rizwan present another stand-up showcase featuring some of the best and funniest new comedians in the UK. This episode features sets from Lloyd Griffith, Joanne McNally and Kemah Bob, while Tom Allen will be trying out some new material

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Host Holly Willoughby and team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh are joined by Aston Villa and former England footballer John Terry, Paralympic gold medallist Jonnie Peacock and Countdown star Rachel Riley. Comedians Romesh Ranganathan and Seann Walsh are on hand too

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: Scooby and the Mystery Inc. gang meet Wanda Sykes, who is helping save a bunch of cute, fuzzy animals up for adoption.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The TV chef is joined by guest Kirsty Gallacher, and chefs Thomasina Miers and Francesco Mazzei, and heads off on a food adventure to Kent. This week's recipes include langoustines, buttermilk fried chicken, mussels and sole, and a masterclass in celeriac

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott puts his own twist on the Great British teatime, serving up black forest brownies with kirsch cream, cheesy chive and bacon muffins and savoury afternoon tea tartlets. He is joined in the kitchen by John Partridge, who bakes some sausage rolls, and Candice Brown, who prepares gin and tonic drizzle cake and rocky road cookies

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's culinary tour of France leads him to the Camargue, a distinct and diverse region located south of Arles between the Mediterranean and the two arms of the Rhone delta. James begins his exploration at Les Marquises, the Laurent family's farm, where he learns all about the Camargue bull. He then barbeques a local fish, which he serves with squash chutney for the family. Later, Michelin-starred chef Armand Arnal shows James around his impressive gardens and lets him pick ingredients for his next dish - stuffed courgette flowers

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of this afternoon's meetings, featuring eight races in total. The feature races of the day are the 2.25 Cumberland Lodge Stakes from Ascot, the 2.45 Sun Chariot Stakes from Newmarket, the 3.05 bet365 Challenge Cup from Ascot, the 3.25 Trophy Stakes from Redcar and the 3.40 Bengough Stakes from Ascot. With analysis by Jason Weaver, reports from Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Rishi Persad, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To help get Brian's life in order, Stewie and Brian swap bodies using a machine that Stewie invents. However, chaos quickly ensues around Quahog when the machine goes haywire

programme.title: Celebrity Karaoke Club
programme.desc: Scarlett Moffatt, Courtney Act, Jess Wright, Luke Kempner, Melvin Odoom and David Potts battle it out in the hope of becoming the ultimate karaoke star - only for things to get even more competitive when comedian Judi Love joins the ranks

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter uses new methods to attract millennials to the brewery he is promoting, which soon garners the attention of a powerful figure in Silicon Valley

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris loses a rare dollar note Carter gave him for his birthday, so the pair begin an adventure to find the dollar, chasing it as it gets passed between every citizen of Quahog

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan is shocked when Agent Crisp from CIA Internal Affairs informs him that Deputy Director Bullock has begun to show early signs of dementia and must have his memory erased. Animated comedy about a man who works for the CIA and is constantly on the alert for terrorist activity

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger declares that Max Jets, one of his alter egos who enjoys spending money on the Smith family, is soon being released from prison. However, things take an unexpected turn when Max falls in love. Animated comedy, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Wendy Schaal

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Rallo is forced to spend a weekend at a retirement home, where he meets a Jewish man and learns about Hanukkah. Meanwhile, Donna encourages Cleveland to stand up to his father. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Mike Henry, Sanaa Lathan and Kevin Michael Richardson

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland discovers Rallo has a natural gift for the sheep-riding sport of mutton bustin' - but Donna learns it is unsafe and orders him to stop competing

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: YouTube stars Alfie Deyes, Joe Sugg and Marcus Butler enter a forest at dusk and endure a series of spooky challenges in order to win cash prizes. Reggie Yates hosts

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Eager to take his team to the high-school baseball state championship, Cleveland persuades Holt to pose as a transfer student in place of his injured star pitcher. However, realising the bad example he has set Junior, the coach tries to clear his conscience

programme.title: The Cleveland Show

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George has already denounced the neighbours as toffee-nosed snobs, and events make Mildred wonder whether he could be right. Queenie Watts and Michael Robbins guest star, with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accidental shooting at Ashfordly Hall prompts Bellamy to investigate reports accusing acting estate manager Richard Bentham of exploiting youngsters for child labour, but his enquiries reveal a far more complicated situation. Peggy discovers a promising new source of revenue when she realises there is a market for guided tours of the area, but her excitement is cut short by a terrifying encounter. Rural drama, guest starring former Emmerdale actor Peter Amory, with Mark Jordon, Rupert Vansittart and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is not happy about Sarah going off to Spain. Social services come to talk with the Cairns family. Kim attempts to seduce Lord Alex

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lord Alex tries to quietly leave Home Farm after spending the night with Kim. Jack and Rachel insist that what happened between them is over

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Deirdre rush to confront Denise. Jamie blames himself for Jack's accident. Alec lays into Fiona for breaking her contract. Fred has a proposal for Rita

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kelly hints to Ashley about them getting a flat. Jack is embarrassed in hospital when Martin gives him a bed bath. Ken visits a solicitor to find out his rights over Daniel

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A commercial decision to turn Midsomer Deverell's memorial garden into a tea shop proves fatal for members of the Inkpen family. DCI Barnaby's investigation unearths more than mere local hostility as he lifts the lid on long-buried secrets. Anna Calder-Marshall and Margaret Tyzack guest star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. Penhale is confused when he is offered a transfer to Exeter, Bert decides to go back to being a handyman, and Louisa and Martin are intrigued when Dr Timoney invites them for an additional therapy session. Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Carry On Jack (1963)
programme.desc: A bumbling midshipman's uniform is stolen by a woman desperate to go to sea, who adopts his identity. Later, the hapless sailor is press-ganged on to the same ship as her. However, while trying to prove who he really is, he has to contend with mutinies, pirates and the Spanish Navy. Comedy, with Bernard Cribbins, Juliet Mills, Kenneth Williams and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A fox-hunt turns sour when a tramp is found dead in the woods, and matters are further complicated by the murder of the chief suspect's father. As DCI Barnaby investigates, he concludes it is only a matter of time before another member of the tight-knit community suffers the same fate. Guest starring James Bolam

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes someone may have conspired to commit murder

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are called away from a team-building exercise to investigate a fatal explosion at a haulage yard in Calham Cross. The property is owned by Territorial Army commanding officer Matt Parkes, the father of a businessman whose ruthlessness in the boardroom is matched only by his behaviour toward women. To complicate matters, the village is also home to a peace protester and a grieving mother - and Barnaby has to decide if any of these locals is capable of arson and murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with Tim Pigott-Smith, Joseph Millson, Niamh Cusack, Judy Parfitt and Nick Fletcher

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: A young woman is murdered in the vicinity of a library and museum owned by a wealthy philanthropist with an interest in global mysticism. It's a complicated investigation, which leads the detectives into a world of mysticism, erotica, drug addiction and tempestuous family dynamics. But as if the case were not enough to contend with, Van der Valk also finds himself fending off office gossip about himself and his newly single colleague Lucienne. Marc Warren stars as the shrewd Dutch detective, with Maimie McCoy and Luke Allen-Gale

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective now has a murder inquiry on his hands as the body found on the moors is identified as 11-year-old Andre Petri. With Kyle Heath still missing, Banks divides his team to search for a connection between the cases, and when post-mortem examinations show Andre was killed by a heroin overdose, it appears the two boys are linked by the illegal drug trade. Starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of investigative journalist Matt Barber is found in a holiday chalet in a remote village, Banks and his team are initially confronted by an overwhelming number of leads. As the detective identifies a connection between the victim and a mysterious death in the 1980s connected to the surviving members of a cult rock band, he must begin to unpick decades of lies. To make matters worse, the deceased man's father is former senior police officer Jack Barber, who is now a candidate in the local commissioner elections - and Morton is convinced he is obstructing their inquiry. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With lawyer David Hornby now a suspect, Cabbot is forced to admit the father of her baby doesn't know about her pregnancy. She then discovers band member Martin Soames' confession in the John Gaunt case file is missing and that Matt Barber's father Jack was the original investigating officer, leading Helen to believe she has the evidence she needs to push the former policeman harder. But when Banks learns of a mysterious tape recording that appears to link Jack with the Crystal Kiss, he realises he's in a race to find it if he is to have any hope of uncovering the truth. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Carry On Screaming (1966)
programme.desc: A sinister boffin, his sultry sister and two genetic misfits kidnap innocent women from spooky Hocomb Woods and turn them into mannequins - but two dim-witted detectives look set to throw a spanner in the works. Comedy horror, starring Kenneth Williams, Fenella Fielding, Harry H Corbett, Charles Hawtrey, Jim Dale, Joan Sims and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A visiting American academic delivers a controversial speech, with many audience members worried his ideas could be used to target ethnic minorities. So when he is found hanged the next morning and Dr Hobson quickly discounts suicide, Lewis and Hathaway realise they have many suspects - from the local anti-racism activist who was first in voicing her disgust at the professor's beliefs to the women who had bombarded him with threatening e-mails. But then another body turns up and the case takes on a new dimension. David Soul and Patrick Baladi guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actress travels across two of the most enigmatic countries in the Caribbean - Cuba and Haiti - to explore, uncover and share the hidden gems they have to offer. Joanna kicks off her adventure in a Havana boxing gym with one of Cuba's rising female boxing stars. She visits the stars of the cabaret at the National Hotel that pre-dates the revolution, and entertains the cigar workers while they roll cigars in their factory. She visits the small town of Hershey, built on the success of the sugar industry, and visits the tomb of Che Guevara before heading off to Hemingway's favourite beach

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Despite Kate being sued after an accident in the cafe, she refuses to back down and Koji is reluctant to get involved. Although Medium is convinced she should just apologise and get it over with, Kate suspects the legal threats may have something to do with Councillor Bone. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the Highlands as he goes in search of Britain's second-largest bird of prey, travelling through the scenic and ancient landscape by a variety of means - but will he be able to track down the elusive golden eagle?

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the pleasures and perils of Dartmoor National Park - the largest and wildest area of open country in the south of England. He discovers it is home to ancient woodlands, high granite tors and vast tracts of rolling moorland, as well as churches, cream teas, llamas and legends

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A commercial decision to turn Midsomer Deverell's memorial garden into a tea shop proves fatal for members of the Inkpen family. DCI Barnaby's investigation unearths more than mere local hostility as he lifts the lid on long-buried secrets. Anna Calder-Marshall and Margaret Tyzack guest star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. Penhale is confused when he is offered a transfer to Exeter, Bert decides to go back to being a handyman, and Louisa and Martin are intrigued when Dr Timoney invites them for an additional therapy session. Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Carry On Jack (1963)
programme.desc: A bumbling midshipman's uniform is stolen by a woman desperate to go to sea, who adopts his identity. Later, the hapless sailor is press-ganged on to the same ship as her. However, while trying to prove who he really is, he has to contend with mutinies, pirates and the Spanish Navy. Comedy, with Bernard Cribbins, Juliet Mills, Kenneth Williams and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A fox-hunt turns sour when a tramp is found dead in the woods, and matters are further complicated by the murder of the chief suspect's father. As DCI Barnaby investigates, he concludes it is only a matter of time before another member of the tight-knit community suffers the same fate. Guest starring James Bolam

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes someone may have conspired to commit murder

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are called away from a team-building exercise to investigate a fatal explosion at a haulage yard in Calham Cross. The property is owned by Territorial Army commanding officer Matt Parkes, the father of a businessman whose ruthlessness in the boardroom is matched only by his behaviour toward women. To complicate matters, the village is also home to a peace protester and a grieving mother - and Barnaby has to decide if any of these locals is capable of arson and murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with Tim Pigott-Smith, Joseph Millson, Niamh Cusack, Judy Parfitt and Nick Fletcher

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: A young woman is murdered in the vicinity of a library and museum owned by a wealthy philanthropist with an interest in global mysticism. It's a complicated investigation, which leads the detectives into a world of mysticism, erotica, drug addiction and tempestuous family dynamics. But as if the case were not enough to contend with, Van der Valk also finds himself fending off office gossip about himself and his newly single colleague Lucienne. Marc Warren stars as the shrewd Dutch detective, with Maimie McCoy and Luke Allen-Gale

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective now has a murder inquiry on his hands as the body found on the moors is identified as 11-year-old Andre Petri. With Kyle Heath still missing, Banks divides his team to search for a connection between the cases, and when post-mortem examinations show Andre was killed by a heroin overdose, it appears the two boys are linked by the illegal drug trade. Starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of investigative journalist Matt Barber is found in a holiday chalet in a remote village, Banks and his team are initially confronted by an overwhelming number of leads. As the detective identifies a connection between the victim and a mysterious death in the 1980s connected to the surviving members of a cult rock band, he must begin to unpick decades of lies. To make matters worse, the deceased man's father is former senior police officer Jack Barber, who is now a candidate in the local commissioner elections - and Morton is convinced he is obstructing their inquiry. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With lawyer David Hornby now a suspect, Cabbot is forced to admit the father of her baby doesn't know about her pregnancy. She then discovers band member Martin Soames' confession in the John Gaunt case file is missing and that Matt Barber's father Jack was the original investigating officer, leading Helen to believe she has the evidence she needs to push the former policeman harder. But when Banks learns of a mysterious tape recording that appears to link Jack with the Crystal Kiss, he realises he's in a race to find it if he is to have any hope of uncovering the truth. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Carry On Screaming (1966)
programme.desc: A sinister boffin, his sultry sister and two genetic misfits kidnap innocent women from spooky Hocomb Woods and turn them into mannequins - but two dim-witted detectives look set to throw a spanner in the works. Comedy horror, starring Kenneth Williams, Fenella Fielding, Harry H Corbett, Charles Hawtrey, Jim Dale, Joan Sims and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A visiting American academic delivers a controversial speech, with many audience members worried his ideas could be used to target ethnic minorities. So when he is found hanged the next morning and Dr Hobson quickly discounts suicide, Lewis and Hathaway realise they have many suspects - from the local anti-racism activist who was first in voicing her disgust at the professor's beliefs to the women who had bombarded him with threatening e-mails. But then another body turns up and the case takes on a new dimension. David Soul and Patrick Baladi guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actress travels across two of the most enigmatic countries in the Caribbean - Cuba and Haiti - to explore, uncover and share the hidden gems they have to offer. Joanna kicks off her adventure in a Havana boxing gym with one of Cuba's rising female boxing stars. She visits the stars of the cabaret at the National Hotel that pre-dates the revolution, and entertains the cigar workers while they roll cigars in their factory. She visits the small town of Hershey, built on the success of the sugar industry, and visits the tomb of Che Guevara before heading off to Hemingway's favourite beach

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Despite Kate being sued after an accident in the cafe, she refuses to back down and Koji is reluctant to get involved. Although Medium is convinced she should just apologise and get it over with, Kate suspects the legal threats may have something to do with Councillor Bone. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the Highlands as he goes in search of Britain's second-largest bird of prey, travelling through the scenic and ancient landscape by a variety of means - but will he be able to track down the elusive golden eagle?

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the pleasures and perils of Dartmoor National Park - the largest and wildest area of open country in the south of England. He discovers it is home to ancient woodlands, high granite tors and vast tracts of rolling moorland, as well as churches, cream teas, llamas and legends

programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.desc: A wealthy widow asks for help when her daughter is kidnapped, but a meeting with the abducter does not go to plan

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar tries to stop a crime ring forming a deadly alliance with psychology students who plan murders. Drama, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A racehorse trainer asks Holmes to investigate the mysterious disappearance of one of his cash-strapped employer's many creditors. Victorian whodunit, starring Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke, with a young Jude Law in an early supporting role

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Britain's most advanced computer suffers a series of acts of sabotage. Steed and Tara try to decode a cryptic message from the machine naming the culprit. Dennis Price guest stars

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the sixth day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, where the third-round matches in the men's and women's draws are scheduled to get under way. Great Britain's Johanna Konta continued her bid for glory on day six in 2019 with a 6-2, 6-1 victory over Viktoria Kuzmova, but second seed Karolina Pliskova's tournament came to an end when she lost 6-3, 6-3 to Petra Martic. The corresponding seed in the men's event, Rafael Nadal, had no such trouble as he defeated David Goffin in four sets, and his great rival Roger Federer joined him in round four after a 6-3, 6-1, 7-6 victory over Casper Ruud. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: An Audience with Billy Connolly
programme.desc: The comedian descends on London to entertain a celebrity crowd including Twiggy, Ringo Starr, Michael Brandon, Samantha Fox and Clive James, covering topics including ageing, parties and his idea for a new national anthem

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Car Crash Global: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dash-cam video footage of a host of astonishing accidents, spectacular smashes and enthralling escapes from Scandinavia and China

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A business deal backfires badly, leaving the future of the Winchester in doubt, so Ray organises a fundraising football match against the police. However, with kick-off looming, one of Arthur's incompetent associates appears with a huge sum of money stolen from a rival businessman, leaving the the dodgy dealer seeking a way to stash the cash without it being noticed. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry becomes embroiled with a fascist Prince while delivering a dispatch case to an address in Rome. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team look ahead to a great weekend of racing, which culminates in Paris tomorrow when Enable tries to win her third Arc De Triomphe at Longchamp

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the seventh day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, where the third-round matches in the men's and women's draws are scheduled to conclude. Simona Halep made short work of Lesia Tsurenko on day seven last year as she continued her bid to retain the title, but three-time champion Serena Williams was beaten 6-2, 7-5 by Sofia Kenin. In the men's competition, Novak Djokovic overcame qualifier Salvatore Caruso in straight sets, as did Gael Monfils against fellow home player Antoine Hoang. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: First Blood (1982)
programme.desc: Vietnam veteran John Rambo is wrongfully arrested in a backwoods town and tested to the limits of his endurance by a brutal deputy. Rambo escapes from his cell and heads out to the woods, and with the forces of the law in hot pursuit he finally has the chance to fight them on his own terms. Action thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Brian Dennehy, Richard Crenna and David Caruso

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, featuring the opening fourth-round matches in the men's and women's draws. Great Britain's Johanna Konta overcame Croatian Donna Vekic 6-2, 6-4 at this stage last year, while Sloane Stephens eliminated 2016 champion Garbine Muguruza. In the men's competition, Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal both prevailed 6-2, 6-3, 6-3 against Leonardo Mayer and Juan Ignacio Londero respectively. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: In the Line of Fire (1993)
programme.desc: An ageing secret service agent, tormented by his failure to save the life of John F Kennedy in 1963, is drawn into a battle of wits with a sinister CIA-trained assassin out to kill the current president. As the day of reckoning draws closer, the agent's nerves are tested to the limit - and nothing seems to be going his way. Wolfgang Petersen's thriller, starring Clint Eastwood, John Malkovich, Rene Russo and Dylan McDermott

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, featuring the opening fourth-round matches in the men's and women's draws. Great Britain's Johanna Konta overcame Croatian Donna Vekic 6-2, 6-4 at this stage last year, while Sloane Stephens eliminated 2016 champion Garbine Muguruza. In the men's competition, Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal both prevailed 6-2, 6-3, 6-3 against Leonardo Mayer and Juan Ignacio Londero respectively. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: In the Line of Fire (1993)
programme.desc: An ageing secret service agent, tormented by his failure to save the life of John F Kennedy in 1963, is drawn into a battle of wits with a sinister CIA-trained assassin out to kill the current president. As the day of reckoning draws closer, the agent's nerves are tested to the limit - and nothing seems to be going his way. Wolfgang Petersen's thriller, starring Clint Eastwood, John Malkovich, Rene Russo and Dylan McDermott

Part-time fitness instructor and disco dancer Selvin meets Donna, who seeks a man who can make her laugh. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hazel Ardern throws the second party in east Cheshire, and hopes her skills as a hostess, combined with her carefully selected menu, will be enough to secure victory. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon becomes concerned that his comfortable lifestyle is having an impact on his professional progress and is stopping him from achieving scientific greatness, so he asks the gang to make life more difficult for him.
